title: Product Lifecycle & Compliance Passport
description: Acceptance scenarios for durable product lifecycle passport packets spanning catalog readiness, product documents, supplier compliance, sustainability assumptions, lifecycle risk, and release governance.
guardrails: The assistant never silently changes product master data, SKU identifiers, categories, divisions, HS codes, EANs, DG fields, temperature handling, supplier links, or product documents.
The assistant never silently publishes product passports, ESG claims, customer declarations, labels, certificates, recalls, supplier communications, or customer communications.
All release, sunset, supplier-change, recall, and customer-facing passport actions remain approval-gated through the action queue and audit trail.

scenarios.name: Create lifecycle passport packet
expected: Creates an AssistantProductLifecyclePacket with catalog readiness, passport evidence, supplier compliance, sustainability passport, lifecycle risk, release checklist, response plan, rollback plan, and leadership summary JSON.
Writes an assistant audit event with objectType assistant_product_lifecycle_packet.

Does not mutate Product, ProductDocument, ProductSupplier, SrmSupplierDocument, SupplierOnboardingTask, AssistantSustainabilityPacket, AssistantContractCompliancePacket, ScriExternalEvent, or customer-facing records.

scenarios.name: Queue product passport review

body.note: Review cold-chain passport evidence before launch.

expected: Creates an AssistantActionQueueItem with actionKind product_lifecycle_passport_review and a link to /assistant/product-lifecycle-passport.
Updates only the packet status/actionQueueItemId to PASSPORT_REVIEW_QUEUED.
Does not activate products, publish passports, change supplier compliance documents, send certificates, make ESG claims, launch recalls, or update customer communications.

scenarios.name: Approve reviewed lifecycle packet

body.note: Approved for product owner follow-up; no source records changed.

expected: Marks only the AssistantProductLifecyclePacket as APPROVED with approver, timestamp, and note.
Writes an audit event proving the approval was human-reviewed.
Does not mutate catalog, supplier, product document, compliance, sustainability, risk, passport publication, recall, or external communication records.
